message:
fix(websocket): route Message::Close through close handshake in send()

Previously, calling send(Message::Close(_)) would encode the close
frame directly without transitioning the handshake state machine,
leaving the connection in an inconsistent state where the peer
received a close frame but the local side still considered itself
open.

Now send() intercepts Message::Close before frame encoding and
delegates to initiate_close(), which properly transitions the
handshake state, sends the close frame, and rejects subsequent
data frames with NotConnected.

Applied consistently to client.rs, server.rs, and split.rs (writer).

Tests (one per module): send_close_message_initiates_close_handshake
verifies that after send(Message::Close(None)):
1. is_open() returns false
2. Subsequent send() returns NotConnected error

Client and server tests use a new TestIo mock implementing
AsyncRead + AsyncWrite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
